Title: Starch synthase 4 is essential for coordination of starch granule formation with chloroplast division during Arabidopsis leaf expansion

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partially restores glucan/starch synthesis and granule formation in ss3ss4, reducing ADP-glucose and improving growth, but does not restore normal granule number, distribution, or morphology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chloroplast-targeted Agrobacterium glgA was active in ss3ss4 leaves. 2. Expression increased starch and growth and lowered ADP-glucose dramatically. 3. Granules formed in only some chloroplasts and were highly variable in size and shape. 4. Thus extra self-priming glucan-synthesizing capacity can bypass SS4 deficiency only partially.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic chloroplast-targeted overexpression; confocal verification of targeting; native PAGE activity staining; metabolite and starch assays; microscopy
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transgene promoter not explicitly stated for pB7WGY2-derived construct
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic expression in leaves of ss3ss4 plants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark growth conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used as a heterologous engineering tool to increase glucan synthesis capacity and partially rescue starch initiation defects, but insufficient to recreate normal transitory starch architecture
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete complementation with abnormal granule morphology and uneven chloroplast distribution
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a functional bypass probe rather than a native plant granule-initiation factor

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Soluble starch synthase family; glycosyltransferase; distinctive N-terminal coiled-coil domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Starch granule initiation/amylopectin glucan elongation module
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Starch glucan chains / granule primer leading to starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate alpha-1,4-glucan chains and is required for formation of the glucan primer/new starch granules in chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transient carbon/energy storage compound supporting nocturnal metabolism and growth; statolith starch supports root gravitropism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf chloroplasts; root cap columella plastids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes coordinated formation of multiple normal flattened leaf starch granules; loss causes severe reduction/delay in granule initiation, very low starch in immature leaves, abnormal rounded granules, and massive ADP-glucose accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ss4 immature leaves contained almost no starch granules and very low starch despite high ADP-glucose. 2. Mature ss4 leaves formed only about one large rounded granule per chloroplast instead of multiple flattened granules. 3. Blocking degradation with sex1 did not restore starch in immature ss4 leaves, indicating a primary synthesis/initiation defect. 4. Chloroplast-targeted bacterial glycogen synthase or enlarged chloroplast volume partially restored granule formation but not normal granule distribution, size, or shape. 5. Inducible SS4 RNAi reduced granule numbers in newly formed immature leaves but not mature leaves, showing SS4 is specifically required during granule formation in leaf expansion. 6. Half-normal SS4 dosage was sufficient, indicating SS4 is essential but not limiting in wild type.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (ss4-1, ss4-3); complementation with 35S::SS4 cDNA; dexamethasone-inducible RNAi knockdown; metabolite profiling of ADP-glucose and starch; microscopy of chloroplast granules; genetic interaction with sex1 and arc mutants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for starch granule initiation and coordination with chloroplast division; essential but not shown to be rate-limiting by dosage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of target product granule primer and initiation of starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; dexamethasone-inducible pOpOff2 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally required in immature expanding leaves during chloroplast division; also affects root cap starch formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf developmental stage; dexamethasone treatment for inducible RNAi; 12 h light/12 h dark growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate transitory starch granule number, morphology, and turnover in leaves; should be combined with mechanisms controlling chloroplast development because SS4 alone is essential but nonlimiting; strong loss impairs growth and root gravitropism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces growth, chlorophyll, night starch turnover, and root gravitropic performance; causes large ADP-glucose accumulation and abnormal granule morphology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer SS4 may provide a specific primer or nucleation center, potentially via N-terminal coiled-coil-mediated protein interactions; direct biochemical priming mechanism remains unresolved
